title: phlogacanthus cymosus
intro: Phlogacanthus cymosus, also known as the Thai jasmine or wild jasmine, is a flowering plant species in the Acanthaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, Phlogacanthus cymosus can be consumed as a tea, taken as a supplement, or applied topically as a poultice or oil, depending on the specific health condition being addressed.
